item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
As someone who has used Pages in the past, I always thought something was threatening about the program.

I appreciate that you created an anomaly that is humorous but still retains the threat before the Foundation finds the proper containment procedures. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What does Gevurah mean as a disruption class? Also, plz no delete Minecraft account

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I made sure to clarify that in the containment procedures, since other folks were confused as well. From the list of esoteric classes:

Item endangers the inner structure of the Foundation and limits the ability of the Foundation to carry out its secondary objectives.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What the fuck is Gevurah?

item.title: +200!!! Also author post.
item.content:encoded:
So guys, we did it. SCP-4645 has finally hit +200, so it's about time I made an author post for this.

Huge thanks to gee0765, DrChandra, and Leveritas for the greenlights and the idea crit, and to Pedantique for the draft crit. It's amazing to have made it this far, and I'm sorry I didn't give my invaluable critters the credit they deserved when I first posted this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think something to consider in this scenario is that there is no canon. The existence of the croutons in Site-15 depends upon whether or not you believe that the croutons are real.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
on the one hand, destroying Sites 01, 07, 17, and 19 would result in a severe loss of personnel, a massive containment breach, and a potential Broken Masquerade scenario. on the other hand, i don't want to shell out $30 for a new Minecraft account.
also, does the Site 15 salad bar have croutons? because that's a major deciding factor for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That’s is, it’s Apollyon. +1

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: It's been a decent time since the creation of this article, but I find myself still agreeing with this comment. It brings a great SCP down just enough to be noticeable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From A Comprehensive List of Esoteric Classes, Gevurah: "Item endangers the inner structure of the Foundation and limits the ability of the Foundation to carry out its secondary objectives." I thought this esoteric class functioned much better as a disruption class, and so I added it in.

Regarding the percentage thing, I might change that in the future.

Edit: Yes, I changed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Gevurah?

the danger of SCP-4645's threats has increased by a factor of approximately 10%.

How do you even measure that?

I like the scip as a whole, but the % thing absolutely obliterates my suspension of disbelief.

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: Threat units.

item.title: Re: +150
item.content:encoded: Fuck you, you don’t know how many pencils I can fit up my nose.
150 is a big number. Citation: you would not stick 150 pencils up your nose.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This may be the worst SCP the foundation has ever had to contain. An absolute monster, deleting everyone's minecraft accounts like that. Disgraceful.

On a serious note, I love this SCP, although I do disagree with the things like 10% more threat. How do you quantify it? Is it 10% more costs in damages or something? It doesn't bother me all that much though because the rest of the article is so good. +1

item.title: ACS Addition
item.content:encoded:
I decided to put the ACS in under a collapsible, so that people who like it will get to see it here, and people who don't won't have to look at it. While I doubt it's an original idea, and it's certainly not revolutionary, I thought it would be a pretty cool way to satisfy both parties, especially since I included a little in-universe comment about it.

Anyway, if you think it's a bad or a good change, please let me know.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting that a recent couple of edits made by contravariant were reverted, as they changed the information present and as such do not count as a grammar/mechanics/formatting correction. Author or staff permission is required for such stylistic edits.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you so much for catching that, I've corrected it now.

I'm glad you enjoyed it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely adore this.

My only suggestion is that the final, most devastating threat fails to give a deadline like the prior threats. Not sure if that's an intentional omission.

A fantastic balance of impotence, incompetence, pettiness, and genuine danger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If that's not a K class senario, I don't know what is. Requesting immediate rollback to Keter classification.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A small price to pay for salvation.

scp-4645:
If my demand is not met, then the Minecraft accounts of everyone in Site-15 will be deleted.

me:
demolishes that Sites 01, 07, 17, and 19 with all of their residents and workers inside.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really thought that would end with everyone at Site-15 being executed to contain this thing, glad it didn't go in that direction.

Well guys, we did it! My first SCP hit a hundred upvotes! I'm honestly so happy right now, this is so exciting!

https://i.kinja-img.com/gawker-media/image/upload/s--ZPF8tzfb--/c_scale,fl_progressive,q_80,w_800/wmwcckpyqsp0lmugq40d.gif

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded: How do you quantify threats? What units do you use? "10% more threatening" seems silly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you so much!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the escalation and reset hurt more than help.
This is a great little skip that starts out really strong with the "no salad bar" thing, where it makes absurd demands with trivial threats. But then it keeps going, and I don't know why. It doesn't need to get stronger; it's got the hook right at the top. I'm much more interested in whether it was deliberately targeting that guy before he smashed it, and how its minor threats can still cause a lot of pain to everyone at the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I found it on the SCP Style Resource here: http://www.scp-wiki.net/scp-style-resource

The formatting for the image looks like this: [[include :scp-sandbox-3:component:image-zoom-2 name={$name}|caption={$caption}|width=300px|align=right]]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me at the salad bar closing, and I slammed the +1 button at the minecraft account deletion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I may ask a question, how did you get that zoom and enlarge feature of your photo? I find it aesthetically pleasing (not necessary but still good) and quite helpful for c'est moi who likes to see these images. Otherwise, this SCP was a blast to read. Both threatening and comical all in the same breath.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But do you think they'd transfer you just like that? It would take time, and by that point, your account is gone. They won't destroy the other Sites, which means you'd have to do it off the books. Just get a few friends from Site-15 together, steal some airplanes, and destroy them yourselves. Find some wrecking balls, hack into the nuclear warheads if they have any…really, the possibilities are endless. Plus, what if Minecraft 2 was released the next day? And what if you could only use it if you had an existing account at the time?

I'd do it.

For Minecraft 2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Added the "click and hold to enlarge" thing, so that it is enlarged more. Didn't want the image itself to take up too much space, so I hope this is an alternative that still improves it.
https://thumbs.gfycat.com/FamiliarAnotherFanworms-size_restricted.gif

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And have four Sites that cost millions of dollars to build be destroyed? I would just request that I be transferred to a different Site (not one of the four listed in the threat obviously) so I can keep my Minecraft account while also having the four Sites not being destroyed. I found a loophole right there! Would you ask to be transferred to another Site so you can keep both the four Sites and your Minecraft account safe as well?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll keep my Minecraft account.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would you rather keep your Minecraft account but have four facilities containing potentially world-ending anomalies be destroyed and everyone inside killed, or keep the facilities with all personnel and anomalies safe but have your Minecraft account deleted? Well, you can just buy another Minecraft account for less than $100, but Foundation facilities cost millions, if not billions, of dollars to build. Also, it only takes a few minutes to purchase and set up a Minecraft account, but it takes many years to build Foundation facilities, including clearing out any rubble and/or acquiring the rights to use the land for construction and making up a cover story when requesting such rights.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh god not the Minecraft accounts

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I need extended testing logs for this thing. Yesterday. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A soul for a soul.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If my demand is not met, then the Minecraft accounts of everyone in Site-15 will be deleted.

The hardest choices require the strongest wills…

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh! You caught my mistake! The Minecraft thing was a holdover from my old plan for this SCP, when they failed to stop it, which resulted in that K-Class threat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Like I said to ManyMeats, I think I'll put some effort into practicing a clinical tone balance. Glad to see you enjoyed it!

item.title: Re: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded:
Thanks a ton! I think a lot of this resulted in an attempt to keep the clinical tone, but I think I overdid it a bit; also, I'll admit that I don't have very much experience with clinical tone, this being my first SCP. I will definitely keep that in mind for next time.

Again, very grateful for your thoughts, and I'm happy that you liked it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If my demand is not met, then the Minecraft accounts of everyone in Site-15 will be deleted.

I am literally playing Minecraft right now and I feel absolutely ATTACKED.

Well done! A malevolent but impotent entity is fascinating to watch. I think you could tighten up some of the language: as ManyMeats said above, the final 1/3 of the piece doesn't keep the same pace as the start.

item.title: Site Crit Team
item.content:encoded:
Hello author! Really good piece, and the first half of this carries it to an upvote for me.

Narratively, I find myself losing interest in the latter half of this piece. Reducing the effects of the SCP down to "10% more threatening" fell very flat and felt like a cheap way out to try to convince me that things are getting worse. There's a lot of extraneous information giving examples of the escalation, in combination with kind of artificial things like the % increase, that robs this piece of some of its tension.

Very solid effort overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would do some good if you could make the image big enough for the readers to see the text. It's a nitpick, but it's annoying when I can't make out what exactly I'm looking at.

item.title: Not my Minecraft account!
item.content:encoded: I love how you threw in the joke at the end to get me to remember this SCP. Might make a joke SCP on this, the overall SCP is very well-done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really simple, but effective and adorable

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If my demand is not met, then the Minecraft accounts of everyone in Site-15 will be deleted.

That's fucking dark man.

+1
